text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet town brimming with autumnal colors, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. Oliver had seen many seasons come and go. One day, he noticed a curious sight—a squirrel nibbling on an unusually large acorn.

"Nature knows best," Oliver murmured to himself before sitting beside it. The squirrel stopped eating mid-chew.

"I have good news," Oliver said gently. "I found just the thing for your kind."

The squirrel looked doubtful. "Anything?"

"A map to the forest's secretest treasure!"

"You've got to be kidding!"

Oliver laughed heartily. "Follow me, and I'll lead you."

They journeyed deep into the woods. As the sun dipped low, the forest filled with golden light. Suddenly, Oliver stopped, pointing ahead.

"There," he whispered.

A cavern! Mossy, charming.

"Inside?" Oliver asked.

"Yes!"

"Let's explore!"

They ventured inside. Sunlight streamed down on
